backend/main.mo: Placeholder Motoko actor file with a comment noting the project uses a Python/Flask/PyTorch backend incompatible with Wasm/Motoko compilation.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report describing two implemented features: replacing the backend API call in GenreClassifier.tsx with a fully client-side TensorFlow.js classification pipeline, and removing the ICP backend actor dependency from the frontend.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries of each frontend source file, serving as a reference index for the project's frontend architecture.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base CSS file defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark color themes including background, foreground, chart, and sidebar tokens.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the React app titled 'Music Genre Classifier' that mounts the app into a root div and loads the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that renders the GenreClassifier page as the sole page of the single-page application.
frontend/src/components/AudioUploader.ts: Drag-and-drop audio file upload component supporting MP3/WAV/OGG with file validation, size display, and a selected-file preview with a clear button.
Animated horizontal bar chart that displays per-genre confidence scores sorted by probability, with genre-specific icons, colors, and CSS transition animations on mount.
frontend/src/components/EqualizerBars.ts: Decorative animated equalizer bar component used as a music-themed visual accent, rendering a configurable number of amber-colored bars with CSS animations.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Full-page layout shell featuring a hero header with banner image, equalizer animation, genre tags, a responsive main content area, and a footer with a caffeine.ai attribution link.
frontend/src/components/LoadingSpinner.t: Loading indicator component combining animated equalizer bars and a spinning ring, shown while audio is being analyzed with configurable size and label props.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) using React Query, and invalidates dependent queries whenever the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider and useInternetIdentity hook for managing ICP Internet Identity authentication state, including login, logout, and identity persistence via AuthClient.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports fonts, configures a dark charcoal/amber Tailwind CSS theme via CSS variables, and defines custom utility classes and keyframe animations (equalizer bars, fade-up, bar-grow).
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that renders the React app wrapped in QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/GenreClassifier.tsx: Page component that accepts audio file uploads, runs client-side feature extraction and genre classification, and displays the predicted genre with confidence scores and a chart.
frontend/src/utils/audioFeatureExtractor: Extracts spectral and temporal audio features (RMS, ZCR, spectral centroid, rolloff, flux, band energy ratios, tempo, dynamic range, flatness) from an audio file using the Web Audio API and a manual DFT implementation.
frontend/src/utils/genreClassifier.ts: Rule-based genre classifier that scores audio against 10 GTZAN genres using extracted acoustic features, applies softmax normalization, and returns the predicted genre with per-genre confidence scores.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration for the frontend, extending the default theme with custom fonts (Inter, Space Grotesk), oklch-based color tokens for UI components, and custom amber/charcoal palettes. Enables dark mode, typography, container queries, and animation plugins.
project_state.json: Tracks the project state (version 5) for a client-side Music Genre Classification web app, documenting existing features, architectural notes (no backend, all ML inference runs in-browser via TensorFlow.js/Web Audio API), and feature implementation history.
